authority_boundary.diagnostic_components: transient non-colliding HISM components tagged RaftSim_ExternalReviewOnly
authority_boundary.fixed_between_modes: camera, exposure, source masks, tree transforms, near/mid/far meshes, terrain, water, rocks, trunk material, collision, solver, and GeoClaw reference

accepted_findings: All four captures retain the same deterministic 1,200-tree placements and the V13/V14 near, mid, and runtime-far representation counts.
Disabling only native leaf shadows removes the long black ground carpets in both views, proving that leaf shadow casting is one independent visual defect.
The opaque leaf override reveals dense crowns registered to the existing trunks in both views, including near, mid, and runtime-far representation bands.
The source-masked HISM placement and retained crown geometry are therefore valid technical inputs for the next material split test.
rejection_reasons: Native masked leaves remain almost entirely invisible after their shadows are disabled, so the corridor still reads as bare trunks on empty slopes.
The opaque green cards are a diagnostic visibility control, not a photoreal material and not eligible for production promotion.
The test does not yet distinguish alpha texture or mip loss from masked cutoff, two-sided foliage shading, or transmission response inside the native material.
Smooth 30 m terrain, provisional flat water, sparse rocks, and absent native understory remain separate lifelike blockers.
next_iteration_requirements: Add a same-shader constant-opacity or alpha-floor diagnostic with leaf shadows disabled to split opacity and mip behavior from TwoSidedFoliage lighting and transmission.

Inspect the project-owned leaf atlas alpha distribution, imported texture alpha preservation, mip coverage, sampler type, opacity-mask clip value, and HISM distance behavior before retuning color or geometry.
Select a bounded alpha-preserving masked solution that restores crown mass without opaque card rectangles, shadow carpets, emissive fill, or synthetic cover geometry.
Repeat guide-seat and river-eye captures before adding the second native canopy species and understory strata.
